none: Man and woman hold hands on couch

body.creator: lucia
body.value: Reynolds says that “a house that doesn’t change is a dead house.” His residence has been closely associated with him in various ways, particularly as his atelier is also where he lives, so here, he is clearly speaking about himself as well as about a more metaphorical understanding of his home and business. Reynolds now sees that for too long he has been closed off and resistant to change. Alma has shown him an alternative—through unusual, dangerous means.

body.creator: lucia
body.value: As the camera slowly pushes in, the wedding dress on the left side of the frame gradually leaves the frame. Reynolds proposes to Alma, who has overcome the “challenge” represented by the Belgian Princess. That wedding dress (now cursed) disappears from sight, both ours and that of Reynolds. Likewise, Reynolds refers to breaking the curse that has plagued his house.

body.creator: lucia
body.value: Alma takes her time to answer Reynolds. She has to consider marrying this man given his demands and the fact that she recently poisoned him, but her delay is also a power move, keeping him in check and signaling that she also commands control in their relationship. The two are shot at angle from which neither is looming over the other, likewise suggesting that they are equals in their relationship.

body.value:
Alma takes her time to answer Reynolds. She has to consider marrying this man given his demands and the fact that she recently poisoned him, but her delay is also a power move, keeping him in check and signaling that she also commands control in their relationship. The two are shot at angle from which neither is looming over the other, likewise suggesting that they are equals in their relationship. 

 

Reynolds’s frustrated “no” echoes the “no” of their first meeting, and Alma also asks Reynolds to marry her—a bold move.
